package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for byte_string_conversion_in_loop: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Byte-to-string or string-to-byte conversion observed inside loops in short-lived lookup or append paths.
type CaseByteStringConversionInLoopDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseByteStringConversionInLoopModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseByteStringConversionInLoopRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseByteStringConversionInLoopModel
}

type CaseByteStringConversionInLoopService struct {
    repo *CaseByteStringConversionInLoopRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseByteStringConversionInLoopAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveByteStringConversionInLoop(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseByteStringConversionInLoopDTO) error {
    lookup := make(map[string]int, len(items))
    var combined []byte
    for _, item := range items {
        lookup[string(item.Payload)] += item.Amount
        combined = append(combined, []byte(item.ID)...)
    }
    _ = lookup
    _ = combined
    return nil
}
